codeReview.summary: The feature correctly introduces a shared effective_policy resolver and threads peer_permission_states into both PoliciesState and PeerRow, but it still breaks cross-surface parity for offline peers.

issues.description: PeerRow suppresses all SIGN/ECDH/PING/ONBOARD badges whenever peer.online is false, while PoliciesState always renders all four policy chips from effective_policy for each configured peer. This creates a deterministic mismatch between PeersPanel and Peer Policies for offline peers, violating the feature requirement that both surfaces show identical per-(peer,verb) policy state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Transcript and handoff verification log use piped test commands (e.g., `vitest ... | tail -N`) while also recording exit codes. This can mask test failures because pipeline exit status reflects `tail`, not `vitest`, unless pipefail is set.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcript skeleton shows multiple commands ending with `| tail -...`; handoff verification records `npx vitest run ... (pre-impl)` with `exitCode: 0` while observation says `4 failed / 2 passed`.

summary: Reviewed feature m3-peer-policies-view using the assigned handoff, commit diff, and transcript skeleton. Result: fail due to a blocking parity gap—offline peers hide PeerRow badges even when effective_policy grants methods, so PeersPanel and PoliciesState can disagree for the same peer/method snapshot.
